title: Banking Shield: Machine-Checked Absence Claims for Privacy-Sensitive AI Explanations
description:
Author-prepared research preprint. Banking Shield is a fictional, non-bank, research-only banking-adjacent prototype whose main artifact is bounded evidence of absence: gate-backed evidence that sensitive values were not recorded in the frozen evidence pack and did not enter the explanation payload. It combines a fail-closed metadata firewall whose rejections become audit evidence, per-session tamper-evident HMAC audit chains with withdrawal semantics, and a deterministic offline AI-style privacy firewall (allowlist input construction, a deterministic mock narrative provider, a negation-aware output claim firewall, per-response privacy receipts, and a static source no-egress gate over four AI-firewall modules). At the evidence freeze all automated gates passed (417/417 unit tests; 43/43 end-to-end checks; 27/27 security checks; three privacy audits) and five trusted internal testers completed 30 formative sessions with zero sensitive values in the evidence and 5/5 checklist comprehension of the system's non-claims.

Non-claims: the prototype makes no fraud-detection, scam-prevention, payment-safety, payee-verification, financial-advice, compliance, or production-readiness claim, and does not evaluate banking effectiveness or live LLM safety. Not yet peer reviewed.
notes: Fictional, non-bank, research-only prototype. Empirical facts trace to the Stage B5 evidence pack frozen at repository commit 92dabb4 and re-audited at 3dcf21b. Substantial LLM assistance was used in drafting and is disclosed in the manuscript. Source: https://github.com/Raoof128/Project-Simurgh
